topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red ink on the paper seemed to bleed into my vision. A C-minus. It was a catastrophic failure, a public declaration of my inadequacy. I stared at the grade, my hands trembling so violently I had to grip the desk edge to steady myself. Every muscle in my body felt heavy, like wet sand. I could feel a strange, cold hollowness expanding in my chest, a sensation that made breathing shallow and painful. I didn't look up when Professor Albright finally cleared his throat, the sound echoing in the sudden, deafening silence of the lecture hall. I just focused on the grain of the wooden table, willing myself to disappear into the surface.

[story 2]

"That's... disappointing, Alex," the professor said, his tone measured but undeniably cool. The words landed like small stones in my stomach, each one causing a sharp, internal clench. I managed a weak nod, my gaze fixed on my worn sneakers scuffing the linoleum floor. My throat tightened, a dry, scratchy feeling that made forming a coherent response impossible. Inside, a frantic buzzing started—a million tiny voices whispering that I wasn't smart enough, that I was fundamentally incapable of grasping anything. I felt a sudden urge to shrink, to fold myself into the chair, hoping the surrounding air would somehow absorb the failing energy radiating off me.

[story 3]

I walked home under a sky the color of old dishwater, the weight of the disappointing mark pressing down on my shoulders. Each step felt laborious, as if I were wading through thick mud. When I finally reached my cramped apartment, I didn't bother turning on the lights. I just slumped onto the sofa, pulling my knees up to my chest. The silence of the room was oppressive, amplifying the frantic rhythm of my own pulse pounding against my ribs. I picked up the crumpled paper, smoothing the creases with a shaky thumb, and then slowly, deliberately, I let the paper fall to the floor, letting it settle in the dust as if it were something toxic that needed to be purged from my immediate vicinity.
